name: Jason Yates
subject: TODAY Unplanned hits theaters!
body:
| | Peter, The film UNPLANNED has the power to influence culture and expose the true goals of the abortion industry. The movie is in theaters today! As one of the youngest Planned Parenthood clinic directors in the nation, Abby Johnson was involved in upwards of 22,000 abortions and counseled countless women on their reproductive choices. That was until the day she saw something that changed everything. Abby Johnson’s story will captivate hearts and change minds on the abortion issue. It’s critical for Christians to support this [movie in theaters](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=bgZjw6Nn__SwItp1FFmBVIvgCsf_Ak5k6UtT-HiwHfQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190329com01&n=1). Invite as many people as possible to go to the movie with you! [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=bgZjw6Nn__SwItp1FFmBVIvgCsf_Ak5k6UtT-HiwHfQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190329com01&n=2) It’s not often that we have a tool this powerful to communicate the truth about abortion in our communities. Besides seeing the movie in theaters, I want to ask you to pray tonight for the tens of thousands of people who will be watching this film across the country. My Faith Votes is thrilled to provide state-of-the-art technology at the end of the film to guide movie-goers to ways they can take action on pro-life issues or access counseling resources if they have experienced abortion in their past. Please pray that this film will move every person. And pray for healing to take place and for people to commit to take a stand for the sanctity of life. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190329com01&n=3)

name: Tina Smith
subject: I'm writing to you personally to explain why this is so important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
We're going to need a strong grassroots effort to meet our goal.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-march?refcode=rtw_20190329_em-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Friend, With the most critical fundraising deadline of 2019 so far just two days away, I wanted to take a few minutes to reach out personally and explain why this deadline is so important to me and my team. I know the 2020 election may seem far off -- but the truth is, we have to start organizing now if we're going to win this top-targeted race next year. Trump lost Minnesota by only 1.5 points in 2016, and the GOP has already confirmed they think they can turn this Senate seat red. That's why we're so focused on meeting these early fundraising deadlines: The only way we'll win this race is by raising early money that allows us to grow our team and build infrastructure all across Minnesota. And in my case, much of that early money comes from grassroots fundraising online -- donors like you chipping in a few dollars when you can. The other side has billionaires and dark money groups lining up to take me down, but we're relying on Minnesotans like you to invest in our shared values and fights. I know the news out of Washington can be discouraging some days -- and this has been a long week. We're continuing to fight to make Special Counsel Mueller's report on the Trump campaign's relationship with Russia available to the public. And Monday's news that the Trump Administration is backing a full repeal of the Affordable Care Act -- which millions of people rely on for their healthcare coverage -- is incredibly dangerous and scary. But in times like these -- during days or weeks with setbacks -- the only thing to do is keep fighting, and the best way to fight back is to invest in our future. Your $5 or $10 or $25 contribution to our campaign is much more than just a few dollars. It's a sign of the values you believe in and the fights you're supporting. It's a sign that even when we face setbacks, we're ready to keep working to make this country stronger. **[So right now, with our end-of-quarter deadline just two days away, I'm asking you to make your first contribution to this campaign and help us keep this Senate seat blue. We have to raise $15,392.10 to hit our goal and put up a strong first quarter fundraising number, and I'm counting on a strong grassroots effort to help us get there.](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-march?refcode=rtw_20190329_em-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=")** Thank you for being on this team with me, friend. I'll be in touch soon. Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory.

Chip in right now to make sure Tina has the resources to fight back. Her first critical end-of-quarter fundraising deadline is this Sunday.** [CONTRIBUTE](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-march?refcode=rtw_20190329_em-full-biobox&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto")

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: I would like to say thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
I'd like to acknowledge and thank a few of the people who have generously contributed to our grassroots campaign this week.
| | Hi Peter,

I am sure that you have been getting many requests from campaigns, asking you to contribute a few dollars to help them for their end-of-quarter deadline. I'll admit, this is one of those emails, too.

**But, before I ask for your help, if you 'll give me a moment, I'd like to acknowledge and thank a few of the people who have generously contributed to our grassroots campaign this week:**
* * *
Renee from Henrico sent in $5.00. _Thank you so much, Renee._

Margaret from Amelia contributed $25.00. _Thank you, Margaret._

Paul from Goochland gave $50.00. _Thanks for helping, Paul._
* * *
Running for Congress isn't easy. Asking you for help, for your time, and for your hard earned money isn't easy. As such, it's so important to me that you know how grateful I am for your support and generosity. People like Renee, Margaret, and Paul help us make our campaign possible.

People are tired of the same politics as usual, and in 2018, **we succeeded because we did things differently! We turned to real people, real families, and real grassroots supporters. I listened to people 's concerns. My team worked hard to build a tremendous network of supporters, and people like you were willing to believe in us, work with us, and help us win.**

Now I am working hard in Washington, and I am proud of my efforts, my work on behalf of people across Virginia, and my voting record. I refuse to play political games with issues that matter to Central Virginia families, like protecting access to health care, ending the culture of corruption in Washington, and improving our local economy. No matter how hard or challenging, I am going to continue working and fighting to make a difference.

[**I want to _maintain_ your trust and your support, Peter. That's why I don't take asking you for a contribution lightly. I hope that you will consider contributing toward our end-of-quarter fundraising goal tonight. We'll only be able to reach our goal with the support of folks like you. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/8OhOyPnrLVm8KLd-7Xo-1l6eNN4gR-eTCzneDAU2Wo0=/REZCUfoqQIj40PcSIC7OD1PBm8aBu2o4LYdEDZoT0U8=?refcode=em_fr_2019.03.29_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/8OhOyPnrLVm8KLd-7Xo-1l6eNN4gR-eTCzneDAU2Wo0=/REZCUfoqQIj40PcSIC7OD1PBm8aBu2o4LYdEDZoT0U8=?refcode=em_fr_2019.03.29_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)
---
I truly appreciate your continued support, Peter.

Sincerely,

_Abigail_
| Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/8OhOyPnrLVm8KLd-7Xo-1l6eNN4gR-eTCzneDAU2Wo0=/7XQpPv5CvoBx3go_PuoUoCzuY43OdxDZ2QS36ck9apg=)

If you'd only like to receive our most important email updates, you may [click here](https://act.myngp.com/el/8OhOyPnrLVm8KLd-7Xo-1l6eNN4gR-eTCzneDAU2Wo0=/Czcpk6Gvm8KW0I6ykGF9yW1KoagmQzYIgz3yt50yqo8=) to request fewer emails from Abigail's campaign.

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/8OhOyPnrLVm8KLd-7Xo-1l6eNN4gR-eTCzneDAU2Wo0=/FxZISP-seFrtXMsx5rWG65zCFYAFFP3matOtdBY8k4E=).

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The Trump-Russia Collusion Hall of Shame
body:
"We have long since passed the point where Americans expect objectivity from the press. But we should hold our elected and appointed officials..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8b098e196&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **The Trump-Russia Collusion Hall of Shame**

“The news that special counsel Robert S. Mueller III ‘did not find that the Trump campaign, or anyone associated with it, conspired or coordinated with the Russian government’ has left a lot of people in Washington with a lot of explaining to do,” Marc Thiessen writes in _The Washington Post_.

The worst offenders are those who had access to classified intelligence and lied that they had seen actual evidence of collusion, Thiessen explains. “We have long since passed the point where Americans expect objectivity from the press. But we should hold our elected and appointed officials handling sensitive national security issues to a higher standard.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5eb8b2e0d8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In a visit yesterday to Georgetown, Kentucky, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump toured the world’s largest Toyota manufacturing plant with Gov. Matt Bevin (R-KY). She carried the Trump Administration’s message “that U.S. businesses have to become more prominent in working with the federal government to boost worker training and skills development,” Grace Schneider reports for the _[Courier-Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ffdfff0a78&e=3ee4985eea)_. Toyota answered the call to action and “committed to boosting the skills of 200,000 workers.”
---
|
---
|
“Catherine Toney began February in prison and ended the month with a job at Walmart after White House adviser Jared Kushner called the Arkansas-based retailer on her behalf,” Steven Nelson writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=728b333902&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Toney, 55, is believed to be the first woman freed by the FIRST STEP Act, which President Trump signed in December.”
---
|
---
| “The Mueller report confirms that the Obama administration, without evidence, turned the surveillance powers of the federal government against the presidential campaign of the party out of power,” James Freeman writes in _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=832db506d1&e=3ee4985eea)_. “It’s time for Mr. Obama, who oddly receives few mentions in stories about his government’s spying on associates of the 2016 Trump campaign, to say what he knew and did not know about the targeting of his party’s opponents.”
---
|
---
| “Support for a southern border wall among conservatives has jumped since President Trump came to office, reaching a new high of 99 percent,” Paul Bedard reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=909f080ca2&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The survey . . . found that support for Trump’s border wall has increased 85 percent since he became president.”
---
|
---
| First Lady Melania Trump “continues to make an impact with her ‘Be Best’ initiative as she made a surprise visit to West Gate Elementary School in Palm Beach County, Florida, to discuss bullying and encourage empathy among students,” Madison Summers reports for _[Independent Journal Review](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=51ac50eff9&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=81f612b89e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1476eecb4a&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=4064d36bc1)
---
|
---

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Can you contribute today to help us reach 1 million donations?
body:
Not me. Us.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)

Sisters and Brothers -
I am about to ask you to make a contribution to help reach 1 million individual donations before Sunday's FEC fundraising deadline. But first, I want to share what this goal means for our campaign.
We set this goal not because of what it says about me – but because of what it says about us.
Every individual donation sends a clear message that not only are we the best campaign to beat Trump, but we are also prepared to stand together and fight for Medicare for All, college for all, and justice for all.
Meeting our huge goal of 1 million donations will ensure we can take on those fights and win. So I'm asking you directly:
[**Can you contribute to our campaign today to help reach an unprecedented goal of 1 million donations before Sunday's deadline?**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10352?refcode=em190329-1-120&t=28&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10352?refcode=em190329-1-120&t=29&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)
The truth is that meeting this goal will be very difficult. We may make it, we may not. But it is very important that we try.
With only a limited amount of time left before the deadline, I hope you will consider making a contribution today.
**[Make a contribution before Sunday's first official FEC fundraising deadline to help hit our goal of 1 million donations.](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10352?refcode=em190329-1-120&t=30&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)**
Never before has a Democratic presidential campaign received this many donations this early in the primary. We have a chance to make history, but only if we stand together.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

* * *

**Make a donation to our campaign today and help us hit our goal of 1 million individual donations before our first, official FEC fundraising deadline comes to an end.** **

[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10352?refcode=em190329-1-120&t=31&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe using this link**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=32&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV). If you want to receive fewer emails, [just tell us here](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=33&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

We have an official FEC fundraising deadline coming up and we're trying to report as many individual donations as we possibly can. [**Make a contribution today before the FEC deadline comes to a close.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer-march-deadline&t=34&refcode2=873_150750_casMMV&akid=873%2E150750%2EcasMMV)

name: Adam Kinzinger
subject: Mueller Report 📎
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kinzinger for Congress
body:
| [ ](www.electadam.com)
---
Friend, It doesn't seem to matter what facts are presented to them — Democrats in Congress are blindly "resisting" our President at every turn. It's just plain crazy to see so many leaders in our country _disappointed_ to learn that Donald Trump had nothing to do with the Russian interference in our elections. Let me repeat: An entire political party is angry that our President isn't treasonous. It's truly a shame. Now that we have Special Counsel Mueller's report, this needs to be a true wake up call for the Left. It's time to move on! Not only because the report showed no evidence of collusion, but also because it's good for our country to put this behind us. President Trump is willing to do that; Adam Schiff and congressional Democrats should, too. But we all know what's coming next: **Democrats will continue spending millions of your dollars investigating the President**. No matter how fruitless their investigations, they will continue to turn lives upside down and take the focus away from making our country better for our kids and grandkids. The only way this circus is going to end is if we work together to restore the Republican Majority in the House of Representatives, which is where so many of these wasteful and abusive investigations are occurring. **I'm going to continue working for the people who sent me to Washington.** That's why I need your support to restore sanity and stop the endless investigations. Will you join me right now? [I'M WITH YOU, ADAM! GET TO WORK >>](https://www.electadam.com/support?e=6d68a9acc7ac3c9a641bdf5d2966b0b3&utm_source=electadam&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2019_03_28&n=1) I need your help as soon as possible. [With a small donation before the major end of quarter deadline](https://www.electadam.com/support?e=6d68a9acc7ac3c9a641bdf5d2966b0b3&utm_source=electadam&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2019_03_28&n=2) (this Sunday), you can show that the will of the American people is more powerful than any political party. Now is the time for our nation to come together and move forward. I hope you agree, and I hope I can count on your support.

Sincerely, Adam
| [ ](https://www.future1st.us/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6vqT3OpPWah76AO06wwwFqJ-xxr4keELV2d4JdHAmLhp&e=6d68a9acc7ac3c9a641bdf5d2966b0b3&utm_source=electadam&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2019_03_28&n=3) [ ](https://www.future1st.us/r?u=7Sn_jlQ3J2wHxMyMtp8UNCDBgQphekXkJc2K3uZrHWcCkDncOztAEg2HCUvnwt8g&e=6d68a9acc7ac3c9a641bdf5d2966b0b3&utm_source=electadam&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2019_03_28&n=4)

body:
I’m currently on the road, en-route to the Elkhart County Lincoln Day Dinner, where I’m giving the keynote address. We’ve been traveling across...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/standing-up-to-the-left?e=1acd9bb3d6)

Standing Up to the Left

I’m currently on the road, en-route to the Elkhart County Lincoln Day Dinner, where I’m giving the keynote address. We’ve been traveling across Indiana attending these fantastic, locally-driven events, and everywhere I go, one of the biggest applause lines of the night is always the same:

We must continue to stand up to the radical left and the socialist agenda they’re driving in Washington.

An excellent example of this socialist agenda is AOC’s Green New Deal. Senator Todd Young gave an excellent speech on the floor of the U.S. Senate (https://twitter.com/SenToddYoung/status/1105936757705633796?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) this month explaining why Indiana simply cannot afford this liberal nonsense that would cost thousands of Hoosier jobs. Well, the Green New Deal came up for a vote in the Senate this week, and guess what? Even Democrats don’t want to be on the record as supporting this monstrosity. That’s why 42 Senate Democrats – that’s right, 42 of them – voted “present” on the legislation. Check out this video (https://twitter.com/senatemajldr/status/1110675494909956096?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) for a good laugh.

Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell is right. The Green New Deal is an attempted “radical, top-down, socialist makeover of the entire U.S. economy,” and it’s up to us and the Republicans we’re sending to Washington to stand up and say, “NO!”

I’ll hope you’ll join me in this fight. I’ll see you on the road and at your local Lincoln Day Dinner as we continue to work to deliver real results for Hoosiers...and not socialism.

-Kyle Hupfer

PHOTO: Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer smiles for a photo with State Senator Mike Crider. As old friends, they both worked together while Chairman Hupfer led the Indiana Department of Natural Resources and Senator Crider oversaw the law enforcement end of the department.
GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS

Local Roads & Bridges:
Governor Holcomb Awards $115 Million to Communities

189 cities. $115 million. Earlier today, Governor Holcomb joined local leaders to give out some BIG checks to counties, cities and towns across Indiana. These checks are matching grants through the Community Crossings program, which helps local communities fund important road improvements projects. And it’s on top of the $2 billion that’s being invested in 2019 in Indiana’s roads through Next Level Jobs (and the $10 billion you’ll see over the next five years).

Check out a map here to see the full list of communities (https://www.in.gov/indot/files/Recipient%202019%20map.pdf?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) awarded grants through this round of funding. Since 2016, the state has awarded nearly $500 million to communities for construction projects through the Community Crossings program.
http://beatthegovernor.mayhem.cbssports.com/brackets/1/218?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge:
Governor Holcomb’s Bracket Lives On!

Last night, the Purdue Boilermakers did it AGAIN! In an overtime, down-to-the-wire win, Purdue defeated the Tennessee Volunteers – just as Governor Holcomb predicted – and will go on to compete in the Elite Eight! And while Governor Holcomb’s bracket (https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=651b6aed28&e=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) is doing very well in the Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge (he’s currently in 19^th place out of 175 entrants), we do have a few Hoosier Republicans whose brackets are dominating in the challenge!

Remember, the top three finishers at the end of our Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge will win either a basketball signed by Governor Holcomb or an item from our Indiana GOP store (https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=88b277a63e&e=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

Get to Know Zionsville Mayor Tim Haak!

It’s a municipal election year in Indiana! Across the state, we’ve got Republican elected officials who are working hard to deliver results for their communities, and one of them is Zionsville Mayor Tim Haak (https://www.facebook.com/TimHaakForZionsville/?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . He was elected mayor of Zionsville in 2015 after serving on the town council, and he’s using his experience with the town of Zionsville and in economic development to be a champion for his community.

Learn more about Zionsville Mayor Tim Haak:

https://www.facebook.com/martin4logansport/?mc_cid=a5d9712dcd&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Q: What special skills or experience do you bring to the Zionsville Mayor’s Office?
A: I have 15 years of financial experience while working at two banks, economic development experience while working at the Indiana Economic Development Corporation and entrepreneurial/startup experience while working at Elevate Ventures, a venture development organization.

Q: What makes Zionsville unique?
A: The vibrancy of the downtown village and Main Street set us apart from most Central Indiana communities. We've been able to maintain the small town feel by giving local business owners the tools they need to be successful and thrive.

Q: What are your biggest priorities for Zionsville if re-elected?
A: We’re focused on continued economic development and investment in the town's road, sewer, and storm water infrastructure, along with investment in our local parks.

Q: What is your favorite memory so far from your time as mayor?
A: I have a lot or great memories that include the development of South Village, the completion of pedestrian bridges at Lions Park and Zionsville Road, and the completion of two roundabouts at Ford & Mulberry Street and Ford & Bloor Lane.

Peter,
In just four days, my team and I will publicly report every penny of grassroots support that we have in our first Federal Election Commission (FEC) report of 2019.
